tests.test_name: Submit button is disabled when the queue is empty
tests.feature: Multi-file mode — submit and batch status card
tests.description: Verifies that a user cannot submit a batch job when no files have been added to the queue.

test_coverage.what_it_checks: Multi-file mode can be turned on by the user
The submit action is unavailable when the file queue is empty, preventing accidental empty submissions

tests.test_name: Submitting a valid multi-file batch shows the batch status card
tests.feature: Multi-file mode — submit and batch status card
tests.description: Verifies that after submitting a batch with at least one file, the user sees a batch status card and the single-file results panel is hidden.

test_coverage.verification_step: Select format and role, add a file, click submit
test_coverage.what_it_checks: A complete and valid batch submission can be initiated
The user receives confirmation that their batch job has been submitted
The single-file results area does not interfere with the batch workflow

tests.test_name: Batch status card shows the job reference and polling message
tests.feature: Multi-file mode — submit and batch status card
tests.description: Verifies that after submitting a batch, the status card displays a unique job reference and a message indicating that results are being processed.

test_coverage.verification_step: Batch status card shows a job_id reference
test_coverage.what_it_checks: The user can see a traceable identifier for their submitted batch job

test_coverage.verification_step: Batch status card shows the polling message
test_coverage.what_it_checks: The user is informed that processing is underway and results are not yet ready

tests.test_name: Batch status card remains visible after switching back to single-file mode
tests.feature: Multi-file mode — submit and batch status card
tests.description: Verifies that the batch status card persists on screen even if the user switches back to single-file mode after submitting a batch.

test_coverage.verification_step: Submit batch and then disable multi-file mode
test_coverage.what_it_checks: The user can toggle the mode after submission without losing their batch status

test_coverage.verification_step: Batch status card is still visible
test_coverage.what_it_checks: The submitted batch job status is not lost when the user changes the input mode

tests.test_name: Batch status card summary row shows format, role and file count
tests.feature: Multi-file mode — submit and batch status card
tests.description: Verifies that the batch status card displays an accurate summary of the submitted job, including the selected format, role, and number of files.

test_coverage.verification_step: Batch status card shows the format label
test_coverage.what_it_checks: The user can confirm which file format was used for the batch submission

test_coverage.verification_step: Batch status card shows the role label 'Product Owner'
test_coverage.what_it_checks: The user can confirm which role was selected when the batch was submitted

test_coverage.verification_step: Batch status card shows '1' for file count
test_coverage.what_it_checks: The user can see exactly how many files were included in the batch job
